code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test09-FEB-2023Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows evidence of significant renal impairment (elevated creatinine, marked proteinuria), hypoglycemia with low HbA1c, and abnormal urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, bilirubin, ketones, protein). No signs of urinary infection. Findings suggest possible acute or chronic kidney disease with nephrotic-range proteinuria and metabolic disturbances; further nephrology evaluation and assessment for underlying causes (e.g., glomerular disease, hepatic dysfunction) are recommended.
